title: Heat-based technology for risk mitigation of pathogenic infection in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems
abstract: A filtration device for use in an air duct system, the device including at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units positioned generally perpendicular to airflow within an air duct; one or more filters in each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units; a heating element; and a set of isolation gate pairs for each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units. Each isolation gate of the pair is positioned on opposing sides of the enclosure unit. Pairs of the set of isolation gate pairs are open when the heating element is off in the corresponding enclosure unit, and conversely the pairs of the set of isolation gate pairs are closed when the heating element is on in the corresponding enclosure unit. The filtration device is configured to disinfecting an air supply by entrapping pathogens from the air on one or more filters.
claims:
Claims 1. A filtration device for use in an air duct system, the device comprises: at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units positioned generally perpendicular to airflow within an air duct; one or more filters in each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units; a heating element; and a set of isolation gate pairs for each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units, where each isolation gate of the pair is positioned on opposing sides of the enclosure unit, where pairs of the set of isolation gate pairs are open when the heating element is off in the corresponding enclosure unit, and conversely the pairs of the set of isolation gate pairs are closed when the heating element is on in the corresponding enclosure unit. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein an outer layer of at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units as well as the sets of isolation gates are made of materials with low thermal conductivity, low heat capacity, high fluidity during mouldings, high heat tolerance, dimensional stability, and high stiffness. 3. The device of claim 2 wherein said materials contains one or more of Bakelite (polyoxybenzylmethyleneglycolanhydride), PEEK (polyaryletherketone), Polyimide (PI), and liquid crystal polymer. 4. The device of claim 1 wherein each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units is configured with a single heating element and a single air filter. 5. The device of claim 4 wherein the single heating element of each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units is attached to one gate of the set of isolation gate pairs and wherein the one gate is an entry gate for the airflow or an exit gate for the airflow. 6. The device of claim 5 wherein the isolation gate pairs are hinged to each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units, and the isolation gate pairs are rotated together for opening or closing each of the enclosures. 7. The device of claim 5 wherein the isolation gate pairs are retractable and move parallel with respect to each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units. 8. The device of claim 4 wherein the single heating element is fixed inside each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units, and each gate of the set of isolation gate pairs is made up of multiple gates, where the multiple gates are opened or closed by rotation along one or several hinges. 9. The device of claim 1 wherein each of the at least two side-by-side sealable enclosure units is configured with at least two heating elements and a plurality of air filters. 10. The device of claim 9 wherein at least two heating elements are separately attached to one gate of the set of isolation gate pairs and wherein the one gate is an entry gate for the airflow or an exit gate for the airflow. 11. The device of claim 10 wherein the isolation gate pairs are hinged to each of the two side-by-side sealable enclosure units, and the isolation gate pairs are rotated together for opening or closing each of the enclosures. 12. The device of claim 10 wherein the isolation gate pairs are retractable and move parallel with respect to each of the two side-by-side sealable enclosure units. 13. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein the set of isolation gate pairs is actuated with one or more linear actuators. 14. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein each gate of the set of isolation gate pairs is made up of multiple gates, where the multiple gates are connected to sets of sliders and connecting rods that are actuated with one or more linear actuators. 15. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein the heating element comprises a heating conductor welded to a mesh. 16. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein the one or more filters are of any kind air filters including high-efficiency particulate air (HEP A) filters. 17. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 wherein temperature setting control of the heating elements is based on sensor signals from points adjacent to the heating element to control the maximum operating temperature of the filter and at a location expected to be the coldest point in the sealable enclosure unit to ensure a temperature inside the sealable enclosure 

unit is raised above the required minimum temperature for disinfection of the fdter based on targeted pathogens entrapped in the filter. 18. The device of any one of claims 1 to 12 further comprises an automatic or programmable temperature controller/thermostat that is used to control a temperature and a duration of entrapment and disinfection periods for each of the sealable enclosure units based on a measured temperature as a process variable and that of a desired/set value of temperature within the sealable enclosure unit. 19. A method of disinfecting an air supply comprising: drawing air into an HVAC system of claim 1; entrapping pathogens from the air on one or more fdters in line with the airflow to create a contaminated fdter; isolating the contaminated filter from the airflow and heating the contaminated filter outside of the airflow to deactivate the pathogens. 20. The method of claim 19 wherein a second filter of the one or more filters is retained in the airflow during the isolating and the heating.
